name: Origin of the Standard Model's free parameters
summary: The Standard Model has ~25 dimensionless or dimensionful parameters that must be supplied as inputs from experiment: 3 gauge couplings, 9 charged-fermion masses (or equivalently Yukawas), 4 CKM parameters, 4 PMNS parameters + neutrino masses, the Higgs mass and VEV, theta_QCD, and (with right-handed neutrinos) Majorana phases. The SM cannot predict any of these — they enter the Lagrangian as unconstrained constants. A theory-of-everything aspirant must derive at least some of these parameters from a smaller set of structural inputs (geometry, symmetry, dynamics) rather than postulating them. This is the aggregate version of individual value-puzzles like the Higgs mass, flavor hierarchies, and the Cabibbo angle; the question is not 'why is alpha = 1/137 specifically' but 'why must the theory take ~25 numbers as input at all'.

characteristic_numbers.note: Counting: 3 gauge couplings + 6 quark masses + 3 charged-lepton masses + 4 CKM (3 angles + 1 phase) + 3 neutrino masses + 4 PMNS (3 angles + 1 Dirac phase) + Higgs VEV + Higgs self-coupling + theta_QCD. Adds 2 Majorana phases if neutrinos are Majorana.
Pre-2000 counting without neutrino mass: same list minus neutrino sector.

principles_stressed.note: Each free parameter is a separate fine-tuning opportunity at the UV scale; the aggregate is the strongest form of 'the SM is not natural'.

history: The parameter-counting framing dates to the discovery of the third generation (1975-1977) which fixed the SM Lagrangian's structure. Cabibbo (1963) introduced the first mixing parameter; Kobayashi-Maskawa (1973) extended to three generations + CP phase. Neutrino oscillations (1998-2001) added the PMNS sector. The 'why these numbers' framing became sharper post-LHC when no BSM derivation emerged at TeV scale. The string-landscape and anthropic-multiverse arguments (Weinberg 1987, Susskind 2003) frame the parameters as environmentally selected; competing programs aim to derive them from compactification geometry (Yukawas from intersection numbers in F-theory) or modular symmetry (Yukawas as modular forms).

references.citation: Donoghue, Golowich, Holstein, 'Dynamics of the Standard Model', Cambridge (2nd ed. 2014)
Burgess and Moore, 'The Standard Model: A Primer', Cambridge (2007)
Cabibbo, 'Unitary symmetry and leptonic decays', PRL 10, 531 (1963)
Kobayashi and Maskawa, 'CP-violation in the renormalizable theory of weak interaction', Prog. Theor. Phys. 49, 652 (1973)
Susskind, 'The anthropic landscape of string theory', in 'Universe or Multiverse?' (Cambridge 2007)
Particle Data Group, 'Review of Particle Physics', Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001 (2024)
